name: Cherry Cranberry Dessert Squares
recipeIngredient: 2 packages (3.4 oz) Instant Vanilla Pudding Mix
1 cup Frozen light whipped topping, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Microwave the pie crust pouch on DEFROST for 20-40 seconds, or let stand at room temperature for 15-20 minutes. Remove the crust from the pouch. Unfold the crust and peel off any plastic sheets. Press the pie crust into the bottom only of a 13x9 pan, cutting to fit. Press seams firmly to seal.
2. Sprinkle the crust generously with a fork. Bake at 425°F for 9-11 minutes, or until light golden brown. Cool.
3. In a medium nonstick saucepan, combine cranberries, sugar, cornstarch, and cinnamon. Mix well. Stir in water and bring to a boil. Cook and stir over medium-high heat for about 5 minutes, or until the cranberries pop and the sauce is very thick and translucent. Add cherry pie filling and mix well. Cover surface with plastic wrap and refrigerate until cool.
4. In a medium bowl, combine pudding mix and milk. Stir in rum extract. Blend with a wire whisk until thickened. Spoon over baked crust. Top evenly with cranberry mixture and refrigerate 30-60 minutes, or until set. Serve with whipped topping.
